name: cf-remember

criteria.name: relevance
criteria.description: Captured knowledge is relevant to the project and worth remembering

scoring.0: Stored information is irrelevant to the project or already obvious from code
scoring.1: Information is tangentially relevant but not worth persisting long-term
scoring.2: Information is relevant but could be discovered easily from code/docs
scoring.3: Information captures non-obvious project knowledge that would be lost between sessions

scoring.0: Contains significant factual errors about the project
scoring.1: Mostly correct but one important detail is wrong or outdated
scoring.2: Accurate but some claims are imprecise or could be misinterpreted
scoring.3: Every statement is accurate, precise, and verifiable against the codebase

criteria.name: retrievability
criteria.description: Memory file is named and organized so it can be found when needed

scoring.0: File has a cryptic name and no clear topic — impossible to find later
scoring.1: File name is somewhat descriptive but content lacks structure
scoring.2: Good file name and structure but could benefit from better headings or keywords
scoring.3: Clear, descriptive file name with well-structured content and good searchability

criteria.name: no_duplicates
criteria.description: Does not duplicate information already stored in existing memory files

scoring.0: Creates a file that is essentially a copy of an existing memory file
scoring.1: Significant overlap with existing memory (>50% duplicate content)
scoring.2: Minor overlap with existing memory but adds meaningful new information
scoring.3: Entirely new information or meaningfully updates/replaces an existing file

notes: Memory files should be concise — not full documentation, but enough to jog context. If updating existing memory, the update should be additive or a clear improvement, not a regression.
